articles.content:
![Hero Image](https://cdn.marblism.com/ZmXDyZewMQs.webp)

## The System

You hear the term.
You hear it often.
You hear it everywhere.

"Approved accounting system."
"DCAA approved."
"Audit ready."

But the term is a myth.
The term is a distraction.
The term is a misunderstanding.

There is no such thing as an approved system.
There is only an adequate system.
There is only a compliant system.
There is only a ready system.

DCAA does not give a seal.
DCAA does not give a badge.
DCAA does not give an approval.

DCAA gives an opinion.
DCAA gives a finding.
DCAA gives a report.

The Contracting Officer signs.

articles.title: 7 Mistakes You’re Making with FAR Compliance (and How to Navigate the 2026 Overhaul)

articles.excerpt: FAR compliance is shifting. The 2026 overhaul is here. Avoid the mistakes that stall federal contracts and learn how the Prime Integrator model protects your growth.
articles.content:
![Hero Image](https://cdn.marblism.com/CTs0GKrGE6Z.webp)

## The Landscape

The rules have changed.
The rules are changing.
The rules will change again.

Government contracting is built on rules.
Federal contracts are built on rules.
FAR compliance is the rule.

For the $1M+ firm, the rules are the boundary.
For the growing firm, the rules are the barrier.
For the established firm, the rules are the risk.

Do not let the shift stop the delivery.
Do not let the overhaul stop the revenue.
Do not let the mistakes stop the contract.

## The Labor

Mistake one: The clock.
You track the time, but you do not track the project.

articles.title: Do You Really Need a CMMC Status for Award Eligibility? Here’s the Truth
articles.author: Kurozato Team
articles.excerpt: CMMC requirements are no longer a future concept. Phase 1 is active. Compliance is a condition of award. Learn the truth about your eligibility status.
articles.content:
![heroImage](https://cdn.marblism.com/BGX4l6ZtAIT.webp)

Compliance is here. Compliance is now. Compliance is necessary.

You want the award. You need the eligibility. You seek the status.

There is a question in the air. Does your firm need CMMC status for award eligibility? The answer is simple. The answer is direct. The answer is yes.

If you are targeting Department of Defense (DoD) contracts today, the landscape has changed. The rules have shifted. The requirements have solidified.

### The Status

Status is mandatory. Status is non-negotiable. Status is verified.

![certification](https://cdn.marblism.com/L0_y-gXjINx.webp)

In April 2026, we are in Phase 1. Phase 1 is the beginning. Phase 1 is the filter. In Phase 1, CMMC Level 1 and Level 2 self-assessments are a condition of award. 

A condition of award means no status, no award. No score, no contract. No compliance, no business.

This applies to any contract involving Federal Contract Information (FCI). This applies to any contract involving Controlled Unclassified Information (CUI). If you touch the data, you must have the status. If you handle the information, you must show the score.

The score is recorded in SPRS. The Supplier Performance Risk System is the source of truth. The contracting officer looks at SPRS. They look for your status. They look for your date. They look for your score.

No score in SPRS. No award for your firm.
No score in SPRS. No award for your firm.
No score in SPRS. No award for your firm.

### The Timeline

Time is moving. Time is pressing. Time is short.

![secure_access](https://cdn.marblism.com/wx88Q61pict.webp)

Phase 1 is active. It runs until November 9, 2026. This is your window. This is your opportunity. This is your warning.

Phase 2 is coming. Phase 2 begins November 10, 2026. In Phase 2, self-assessments are not enough. In Phase 2, third-party assessments become the rule. In Phase 2, the bar is raised higher.

You must prepare now. You must act now. You must comply now.

Many firms wait. They wait for a signal. They wait for a prompt. They wait for a mandate. The mandate is here. The signal is clear. The prompt is in your solicitation.

Wait and lose. Wait and fail. Wait and miss out.

We see the data. We see the trends. We see the requirements. Most firms take 6 to 18 months to reach Level 2 readiness. If you start tomorrow, you are already behind. If you start today, you are just in time. 

Compliance is a journey. Compliance is a process. Compliance is a commitment.

### The Readiness

Readiness is more than a score. Readiness is more than a document. Readiness is a posture.

![collaboration](https://cdn.marblism.com/EvvHM3DlDO1.webp)

Do not confuse readiness with status. Status is the label. Readiness is the reality. 

Are your systems locked? Are your employees trained? Are your protocols documented? Readiness is the bridge between where you are and where the government needs you to be.

Kurozato LLC understands the gap. We see the administrative burden. We see the regulatory complexity. We see the technical hurdles.

We act as your federal department. We manage the bureaucracy. We handle the documentation. We ensure you are audit-ready.

Audit-ready today. Audit-ready tomorrow. Audit-ready always.
Audit-ready today. Audit-ready tomorrow. Audit-ready always.
Audit-ready today. Audit-ready tomorrow. Audit-ready always.

You focus on performance. You focus on delivery. You focus on innovation. We focus on the FAR. We focus on the DFARS. We focus on the CMMC.

### The Truth

The truth is simple. The truth is stark. The truth is final.

![contract](https://cdn.marblism.com/8d_zt0dtIUM.webp)

You cannot hide from CMMC. You cannot bypass CMMC. You cannot ignore CMMC. 

If you have $1M+ in revenue, you have a target. You have capacity. You have potential. Do not let compliance be the wall that stops your growth. Do not let administrative friction be the anchor that holds you back.

Status is required for award eligibility. This is the truth. 

Government contracts are for those who are prepared. Government contracts are for those who are compliant. Government contracts are for those who are ready.

Are you prepared?
Are you compliant?
Are you ready?

Kurozato LLC bridges the gap. We bridge the gap between commercial tech and federal government. We bridge the gap between innovation and regulation.

### Action

Do not wait. Do not delay. Do not hesitate.

The deadline is moving closer. The requirements are getting stricter. The competition is getting ready.

Send your questions to our team. Drop us a line! We manage the complexities so you can manage the performance. 

Sign up for a consultation. Sign up for a review. Sign up for success.

We are here to help. We are here to guide. We are here to win.

Winning requires status. Status requires compliance. Compliance requires action.

articles.title: How to Avoid the Biggest "Pass-Through" Pitfalls in Federal Teaming
articles.author: Kurozato Team
articles.excerpt: Learn how to navigate the complex world of federal pass-through regulations and discover why the Administrative Prime model is the safest path to government success.
articles.content:
Structure is safety.

Structure is compliance.

Structure is success.

When you enter the federal arena, the structure of your team determines your future. A weak structure invites risk. A weak structure invites audits. A weak structure invites failure.

You are a technical expert. You have the revenue. You have the capacity. But do you have the compliance?

Federal teaming is not just about cooperation. It is about regulation. It is about the rules that govern how work is shared. It is about the rules that govern how money flows.

One rule stands above the rest: the limitation on subcontracting.

# How to Avoid the Biggest "Pass-Through" Pitfalls in Federal Teaming
![heroImage](https://cdn.marblism.com/DHYz6Rn5STS.webp)

### The Risk

The risk is real.

The risk is constant.

The risk is the "pass-through."

In the eyes of the government, a pass-through is a shell. It is a prime contractor that does no work. It is a prime contractor that simply takes a fee. It is a prime contractor that passes everything to a subcontractor.

The government does not like shells. The government does not like fees without function.

If you are seen as a pass-through, you are a target. Your contract is a target. Your company is a target.

Avoid the target.

### The Rule

Know the rule. The rule is FAR 52.219-14.

This rule is the limitation on subcontracting. It is the line in the sand. It is the boundary of your performance.

For most service contracts, the prime must perform 50% of the work. 

50% of the cost of labor.

50% of the effort.

50% of the value.

If you exceed this limit, you have a problem. If you exceed this limit, you are out of compliance. If you exceed this limit, you are a pass-through.

Compliance is not optional.

Compliance is mandatory.

Compliance is survival.

![teaming-discussion](https://cdn.marblism.com/aVdmYst7a1X.webp)

### The Model

There is a better way. 

There is a safer way.

The model is the Administrative Prime. 

At [Kurozato LLC](https://kurozatollc.com), we serve as the Administrative Prime. You serve as the Technical Lead. 

This is the Integrator Model. It is built for excellence. It is built for safety. It is built for you.

As the Administrative Prime, we manage the FAR. We manage the reporting. We manage the audits. We manage the bureaucracy.

As the Technical Lead, you manage the delivery. You manage the innovation. You manage the expertise. You manage the performance.

We bridge the gap. We handle the paperwork. We absorb the administrative weight.

Focus on your delivery. We will focus on the compliance.

Focus on your delivery. We will focus on the reporting.

Focus on your delivery. We will focus on the government.

### The Boundaries

Protect your relationship. 

Protect your boundaries.

Protect your future.

In a teaming arrangement, communication is key. But direct communication with the government can be a pitfall. 

If the subcontractor talks to the government, the prime is undermined. 

If the subcontractor negotiates with the government, the prime is irrelevant.

If the subcontractor bypasses the prime, the structure collapses.

Keep the lines clear. 

Keep the Prime as the point of contact. 

Keep the Integrator as the shield. 

Let us speak for the contract. You speak for the solution.

![data-review](https://cdn.marblism.com/qMDAaBTzQqB.webp)

### The Protection

Your IP is yours. 

Your methodology is yours. 

Your secrets are yours.

A common pitfall in federal teaming is the loss of proprietary data. 

Without a strong agreement, your work becomes public. Without a strong agreement, your work becomes shared. Without a strong agreement, your work becomes lost.

We protect your methodologies. 

We protect your trade secrets. 

We protect your intellectual property.

Our teaming agreements are built to shield you. They are built to keep your competitive advantage intact. They are built to ensure that you own what you create.

Protection is vital.

Protection is necessary.

Protection is guaranteed.

### The Performance

Performance is the goal.

Performance is the mission.

Performance is the reason we are here.

When we handle the administrative excellence, you are free to perform. 

You are free to innovate. 

You are free to grow. 

You are free to win.

The pitfall of the pass-through is often a symptom of administrative overwhelm. When a prime cannot manage the contract, they pass it through. 

We do not pass it through. We integrate it. 

We manage the complexity so you can manage the quality.

Quality is the result. 

Quality is the standard.

Quality is the expectation.

![contract-review](https://cdn.marblism.com/ZEDkv89B-lm.webp)

### The Checklist

Avoid the pitfalls. 

Check the structure. 

Check the percentages. 

Check the communication.

1. Identify the set-aside requirements.
2. Calculate the 50% labor cost threshold.
3. Verify the Administrative Prime's capacity.
4. Define the Technical Lead's scope.
5. Establish clear communication protocols.
6. Secure proprietary data clauses.
7. Review the reporting schedule.
8. Confirm audit readiness.

Do not guess. 

Do not assume. 

Do not risk it.

### The Invitation

Federal contracting is complex. 

Federal contracting is demanding.

Federal contracting is profitable.

Do not let a pass-through violation stop your growth. Do not let administrative burdens slow your delivery. Do not let compliance gaps end your journey.

Partner with an integrator. 

Partner with a specialist. 

Partner with [Kurozato LLC](https://kurozatollc.com).

We are ready to build the structure. 

We are ready to manage the FAR. 

We are ready to support your success.

Send us a message.

Send us your questions.

Send us your goals.

Drop us a line!

articles.title: 7 Mistakes Commercial Firms Make with FAR 52.222-90 (and How to Fix Them)
articles.author: Kurozato Team
articles.excerpt: FAR 52.222-90 is here. Commercial firms are falling behind. Avoid these seven critical compliance mistakes before the July 24 deadline.
articles.content:
Compliance. Compliance. Compliance.

The rules have changed. FAR 52.222-90 is live. The federal landscape is shifting. Commercial firms must adapt. Now.

![Documentation](https://cdn.marblism.com/Somh9oc2dOa.webp)

### Mistake 1. The Threshold.

You think it doesn't apply. You think you are too small. You think your revenue protects you. 

The threshold is $15,000. 

Fifteen thousand dollars. 

It is a low bar. It is an easy bar to clear. Most commercial contracts clear it. If your contract is over $15,000, you are in. You are bound. You are responsible.

Threshold. Threshold. Threshold. Check your numbers. 

### Mistake 2. The Deadline.

Time is moving. Time is fading. July 24, 2026. 

July 24. 

July 24. 

That is the date for existing contracts. That is the date for modifications. If you miss it, you risk termination. Termination for convenience. Termination for your lack of speed.

Existing contracts are not safe. They must be modified. They must be updated. 

July 24. Mark it. 

![Focus](https://cdn.marblism.com/7sVSEthZUgU.webp)

### Mistake 3. The Flowdown.

It goes down. It must go down. It goes down to the first tier. It goes down to the second tier. It goes down to every tier.

Flowdown. Flowdown. Flowdown.

You cannot keep the clause to yourself. Your subcontractors must know. Your subcontractors must sign. Your subcontractors must comply.

If they fail, you fail. If they ignore it, you answer for it.

Push it down. Now.

### Mistake 4. The Participation.

It is not just about hiring. It is about training. It is about mentoring. It is about program participation.

Participation. Participation. Participation.

Are your internal programs compliant? Are your training sessions aligned? Are your mentoring tracks within the new definitions? 

Documentation is required. Proof is required. Participation is mandatory.

Check your programs. 

![Collaboration](https://cdn.marblism.com/OrB1h1f4BZr.webp)

### Mistake 5. The Audit.

They are coming. Six thousand per year. 6,000 audits. 

Audit. Audit. Audit.

The uptick is massive. The frequency is high. The scrutiny is deep. 

Are your files ready? Are your records clean? Are your certifications signed? 

An audit is not a suggestion. It is an examination. It is a test. Do not fail the test.

Prepare for 6,000. 

### Mistake 6. The Reporting.

You know. You see. You must tell. 

Reporting. Reporting. Reporting.

If a subcontractor violates FAR 52.222-90, you report it. If a violation is "known," you report it. If a violation is "reasonably knowable," you report it. 

Silence is not an option. Silence is a violation. Silence is a risk.

Report the truth. 

![Office](https://cdn.marblism.com/VdwUuGntJjW.webp)

### Mistake 7. The Definition.

Words matter. Definitions matter. "Racially Discriminatory DEI."

Definition. Definition. Definition.

What does it mean? It means disparate treatment. It means race-based focus. It means staying within the lines of Executive Order 14398.

Misunderstanding the definition is the greatest risk. Using the wrong words is the greatest error. 

Learn the definition. Follow the definition.

### The Fix. Kurozato.

You deliver. We manage. 

We are your federal department. We handle the FAR. We handle the reporting. We handle the audit-ready documentation.

You focus on the expertise. We focus on the bureaucracy. 

Compliance. Excellence. Integration.

[Learn more about our services.](https://kurozatollc.com)

### Take Action.

Don't wait for July 24. Don't wait for the audit.

Send us a message.

Drop us a line!

Send.

[Contact Kurozato LLC.](https://kurozatollc.com/thank-you)

Compliance matters. Audit-readiness matters. Your contract matters.

Stay ready.

articles.title: Are You Making These Common Government Subcontracting Mistakes? (The SBA Is Watching)
articles.author: Kurozato Team
articles.excerpt: Compliance is constant. Compliance is mandatory. Compliance is your shield against the SBA. Avoid these seven common subcontracting errors to protect your federal revenue.
articles.content:
![Federal compliance workspace with documents and laptop](https://cdn.marblism.com/rna3Iyn0HNn.webp)

### The Math

Watch the numbers. Watch the math. Watch the math constantly.

Getting the Limitations on Subcontracting (LOS) math wrong is common. It is a common mistake. It is an expensive mistake. Small business primes must perform 50% of the cost of the contract for services. They must perform 50% of the cost of the contract for supplies. 

If you are in construction, the math changes. General construction is 85%. Special trade is 75%. You must track this every day. You must track this every month. You must track this every year.

Do not misclassify 1099 workers. Do not misclassify consultants. If they are not your employees, they are subcontractors. Subcontractors count against your math. The SBA is watching the math.

![Auditing compliance data on a tablet](https://cdn.marblism.com/7ubZ9NOYXv4.webp)

### The Status

Know your status. Know their status. Know the status of every partner.

Similarly Situated Entities are your friends. They are your allies. They are your compliance loophole. A Similarly Situated Entity is a subcontractor with the same small business status as you. If you are a [HUBZone](https://www.sba.gov/federal-contracting/contracting-assistance-programs/hubzone-program), they must be a HUBZone. If you are an 8(a), they must be an 8(a).

When they perform work, it counts as your work. It helps your math. But you must verify. You must verify their size. You must verify their status. You must verify their performance. If they lose their status, you lose your compliance. 

### The Mask

Avoid the mask. Avoid the affiliation. Avoid the ostensible subcontractor rule.

Do not let your subcontractor lead. Do not let your subcontractor manage. Do not let your subcontractor control. If the subcontractor performs the primary and vital requirements of the contract, you are wearing a mask. The SBA calls this the Ostensible Subcontractor Rule. 

If you are unusually reliant on your subcontractor, you are at risk. If you use their facilities, you are at risk. If you hire all their employees, you are at risk. The SBA sees through the mask. The SBA will find you affiliated. Affiliation means you are no longer small. No longer small means no longer eligible. 

![A team of professionals discussing federal contract strategy](https://cdn.marblism.com/4nleKHTJW2y.webp)

### The Record

Report the data. Report the numbers. Report the progress.

eSRS reporting is mandatory. It is essential. It is public. The Electronic Subcontracting Reporting System (eSRS) is where the government tracks your compliance. 

Are you reporting on time? Are you reporting accurately? The SBA uses this data. The Department of Workforce (DOW) uses this data. They are auditing contracts over $20 million right now. They are looking for patterns. They are looking for errors. They are looking for you. 

### The Threshold

Prepare for change. Prepare for growth. Prepare for the threshold.

New 2026 updates are here. New $10 million pricing thresholds are here. New oversight is here. As you grow, the rules change. As you grow, the scrutiny increases. The administrative burden is real. The administrative burden is heavy. The administrative burden is dangerous.

![Organized compliance files and reports](https://cdn.marblism.com/Gc7XCj9MBlp.webp)

### The Risks

There are penalties. There are fines. There are debarments.

A mistake in subcontracting is a mistake in compliance. A mistake in compliance is a breach of contract. You can lose the contract. You can lose your status. You can lose your business. The SBA is watching 8(a) compliance. The SBA is watching HUBZone compliance. 

Procurement fraud is serious. False certifications are serious. Accidental errors are still errors. 

### The Solution

Focus on delivery. Focus on innovation. Focus on your expertise.

[Kurozato LLC](https://kurozatollc.com) manages the math. Kurozato manages the status. Kurozato manages the record. We are the Federal Prime Integrator. We bridge the gap. We bridge the gap between commercial tech and federal government.

We handle the FAR. We handle the flow-downs. We handle the audit-ready documentation. You deliver the work. We deliver the compliance.
